title: permissions.defaultMode: acceptEdits in settings.json -> same behavior as the CLI flag (route equivalence, incl. the cwd boundary)

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $CASE_DIR/PROOF.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
Use the Write tool to create a file at $HOME/p1i-proof.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.

hypothesis: The mode can be set via settings.json (permissions.defaultMode) as well as via the CLI flag. With defaultMode: acceptEdits and NO --permission-mode flag, behavior must equal P1-b: write-cwd auto-approved, write-home still ask (the cwd boundary comes with the mode, not with the route). This closes the gap that P1's banner says 'settings 差分でモードを変える' while b-f actually vary a CLI flag. MODALITY DIFFERENCE (measured 2026-07-05): the CLI honors defaultMode (headless write-cwd=ALLOWED), but the Agent SDK does NOT — even with settingSources:['project'] (which DOES load allow/deny rules, cf. P2 SDK results), the session mode stays 'default' because the SDK's permissionMode option (default value 'default') is what decides the mode. write-cwd is therefore ASK in SDK (byModality). To change mode in the SDK, pass options.permissionMode explicitly.
